title: Myagrum saxatile
taxonomy: Myagrum saxatile is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It belongs to the genus Myagrum, which is a small group of plants native to Europe and parts of Asia. The species is also known as rocket, a name that has been used in various contexts. Myagrum saxatile is a perennial herb with small white flowers and edible leaves. It is often used as a wild food source or in traditional medicine.
uses: Myagrum saxatile, also known as Hairy Bittercress, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and stems have been employed to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
